home.metaTitle: Tab Memory — Save browser tabs with context
home.metaDescription: Chrome extension: save tabs with a screenshot and note, close them without losing context. 100% local storage.
home.heroLead: Tab Memory saves a screenshot, your note, URL, and page title — then closes the tab. No more tab hoarding. No more forgotten bookmarks.
home.problemTitle: The problem with too many tabs
home.problemSubtitle: You want a clean browser, but closing tabs feels risky. Ordinary bookmarks pile up and you forget why you saved them.
home.without1: Dozens of open tabs slow you down
home.featuresSubtitle: Built for researchers, developers, and tab hoarders.
home.f1Text: Captures what was on screen when you saved — not just a favicon and title.
home.f2Text: Write why you opened the tab: task, quote, reminder — up to 2000 characters.
home.f3Text: One action: store everything locally and close the tab immediately.
home.f4Text: Browse saved tabs with previews. Sort by date or title. Open or delete.
home.f5Text: No account, no cloud, no sync. Data stays in your browser’s IndexedDB.
home.f6Text: Follows your system appearance — comfortable in any lighting.
home.howSubtitle: Three seconds to save a tab with full context.
home.step1Text: Click the Tab Memory icon on any tab you want to save.
home.step2Text: Optionally describe why this tab matters — future you will thank you.
home.step3Text: Screenshot is stored, metadata saved, tab closed. Done.
home.step4Text: Open History, find the preview, click to reopen the URL in a new tab.
home.mockLabel: Why did you open this tab?
home.mockSample: Compare pricing models for Q2 report…
home.historySubtitle: Your personal tab archive — stored only on this device.
home.h1Text: Each saved tab shows its screenshot, title, date, and note.
home.h2Text: Order by date (newest/oldest) or title (A–Z / Z–A).
home.h3Text: See how many tabs you saved and how much disk space they use.
home.faq1q: Does Tab Memory sync across devices?
home.faq1a: No. Everything is stored locally in your browser. We intentionally avoid cloud sync to keep your browsing context private.
home.faq2a: Screenshot (JPEG), page URL, page title, your note, and save timestamp. Nothing is sent to any server.
home.faq3a: No. Browser internal pages cannot be captured for security reasons. Regular web pages work fine.
home.faq4a: Open History and click “Clear history”, or uninstall the extension. Both remove local data from IndexedDB.
home.privacyCtaTitle: Your data stays on your device
home.privacyCtaText: Tab Memory collects nothing. No analytics, no accounts, no third-party sharing. Read the full policy for Chrome Web Store compliance.

privacy.metaDescription: Privacy Policy for the Tab Memory browser extension.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Tab Memory is a browser extension that helps you save open tabs with a screenshot and a short note before closing them.
privacy.collectText: None. Tab Memory does not collect, transmit, or sell any personal data.
privacy.storedIntro: All information stays only on your computer in the browser’s local storage (IndexedDB):
privacy.stored1: Screenshot of the tab at save time
privacy.storedOutro: This data is not synced to our servers or third parties. Uninstalling the extension or using “Clear history” removes saved data from your browser.
privacy.perm1: Capture a screenshot when you click “Save”
privacy.perm2: Read tab title/URL, close the tab, open history and saved URLs
privacy.perm3: Screenshot and reopen pages on any site you choose to save
privacy.contactText: For questions about this policy, see the Support page or GitHub Issues.

support.metaDescription: Support and contact for Tab Memory browser extension.
support.title: Support
support.lead: Questions, bugs, or feedback about Tab Memory.
support.githubText: Report issues and feature requests on GitHub Issues.
support.storeText: After publication, use the support link on the extension’s store listing page.
